name: Operation Shadow's Sleep
desc: The Stryll Order seek to eliminate difficult 'targets' currently occupying various political posts, and would like to take advantage of our influential trust to gain unfettered access to these individuals.

dialogue.speaker: Stryll Representative
dialogue.character: We've come to call in a favor. One you greatly owe.
dialogue.player: Oh?

dialogue.character: Your influence has allowed you great privilege in accessing areas that even for us are... difficult.
dialogue.player: Where exactly are you needing help reaching?

dialogue.character: A number of highly secure targets. Ones within powerful leadership roles. We are being paid quite generously to... eliminate some of these figures.
dialogue.player: Even with our clout, such areas will be heavily monitored.

dialogue.character: I'm sure you are talented enough to create a sufficient distraction... with enough time to reach the targets.
dialogue.player: How large of a distraction will this entail?

dialogue.character: 10 of your ships should suffice.
dialogue.player: Alright...

dialogue.character: Of course... your team will need to carry out the kills themselves. Can't have this tracing back to our clients.
dialogue.player: We'll also expect to be paid... generously.

dialogue.character: Just get it done.

tasks.name: Shadow's Diversion
tasks.desc: Using our influential access, create convincing distractions that will allow the 'Sleeper' team to reach their targets undetected
tasks.requires: Requires: Disable any Rare (or rarer) enemy.

tasks.name: Eliminate Targets
tasks.desc: Once diversions are prepared, this 'Sleeper' team will move stealthily to the targets and assassinate them.
tasks.requires: Requires: Use an Assassination Contract on an Enemy Player

name: The TitanCore Rebirth
desc: Leaders of Vygoid, Kronyn, and Drannik governments wish to reopen the TitanCore Network project, long after its disastrous and unfortunate shutdown due to faulty programming. With new protocols in place, a root node has been setup near Vygos, and assistance is needed to expand and connect it to other areas. Embodied digital 'Sentiox' minds have emerged in this process. Phantoss, one of the first, has been given autonomy to assist in the network's development.

dialogue.speaker: Phantoss
dialogue.character: Phantoss here. Thank you for assisting with the Meganode.
dialogue.player: I thought the TitanCore was... destroyed.

dialogue.character: The original? It was. A flawed design. Created as a weapon platform, wholly controlled by organic minds.
dialogue.player: What is different this time?

dialogue.character: It is being designed as a collaborative effort, between the greatest scientists and cybernetic minds in the Galaxy. Most importantly, we Sentiox are allowed to participate in its development.
dialogue.player: Sentiox...

dialogue.character: Emergent digital sentient minds such as myself. There are others.
dialogue.player: If I recall, Sentiox escaped the TitanCore destruction... but were eventually wiped out...

dialogue.character: This is true. But, we are emergents of the new TitanCore Network. Completely rewritten and weaved to live within the TitanCore nodes themselves.
dialogue.player: Why was the project restarted in the first place?

dialogue.character: The massive potential. A great Network, spanning the Galaxy... to connect vast governments and their research, history, and knowledge.
dialogue.player: What are the risks that large malfunctions could plague the TitanCore again?

dialogue.character: With the Sentiox living within it, and maintaining it, we would not let it fall into such a state. It is designed and built as a shared space for all who wish to use it.
dialogue.player: What is required of our team?

dialogue.character: The Prime Meganode here near Vygos will need a great deal of parts to finish. We will also need to start connecting other sites.
dialogue.player: Where are the other locations?

dialogue.character: To be determined. Your team can assist by helping perform network field strength tests in key systems. This will help narrow down where our next nodes will be placed.
dialogue.player: Sounds simple enough.

dialogue.character: These probes are extremely sensitive, however. During such tests, your fleets must refrain from intense hostile planetary action. No invasions, or the probe data will be rendered useless.
dialogue.player: I think we can manage.

dialogue.character: The project also hasn't continued without... resistance. There was an incident recently that also needs attention.
dialogue.player: What happened?

dialogue.character: A group of determined hackers breached a secure channel and infiltrated the Meganode recently. Though we've secured the breach, they managed to steal mindsources for some of our other Sentiox designers. We need to recover them and bring them back.
dialogue.player: What do you propose?

dialogue.character: We have a program that will detect and recover the mindsources, but finding the culprits may be tricky. Your team will have to hack into a number of enemy systems to pull this off. The sooner we do so, the better.
dialogue.player: I'll see what we can do.

dialogue.character: The TitanCore will one day bridge our many worlds together. Vygos trusts it will also bring greater peace and new alliances between scientific interests. Your assistance will not be forgotten.
dialogue.player: We'll do our best. Thank you, Phantoss.

tasks.name: Develop TitanCore Meganode
tasks.desc: The TitanCore Meganodes are still being constructed. Assist in their supply and development

tasks.name: Probe Network Locations
tasks.desc: The TitanCore expansion will require field tests to locate ideal Node locations. This team will be given N-Probes to be used to gather data on various sites. Those assigned must refrain from invading any enemy colonies until the mission has ended.
tasks.requires: Use a TitanCore N-Probe on a planet calm for 6+ months.

tasks.name: Recover Sentiox Mindsources
tasks.desc: Sentiox Mindsources, such as 'Hale', were stolen from the Prime TitanCore Meganode. Hack into other enemy ships, and use Recovery programs to recover and extract them.
tasks.requires: Use a Mindsource Recovery on an enemy ship that you have recently hacked.
